title: IACUC — Animal Research Available Equipment
content:
The Morgan State University Animal Research Facility (ARF) provides a broad suite of surgical, histology, behavioral testing, and molecular-laboratory equipment for investigators conducting animal studies. Equipment is organized by functional area as follows:

--- Surgery ---
Surgery room equipped with two small-animal surgery tables (one backdraft and one downdraft), digitized stereotaxic instruments, a gas and chemical anesthesia system, a dry-ice machine, animal physiological monitoring for vital signs (Kent Scientific), and an autoclave system for surgical sterilization.

--- Histology ---
Biosafety Level II cabinet, chemical fume hood for perfusions, HistoCore Arcadia tissue processing and embedding apparatus, Leica RM2255 fully automated rotary microtome, hot plates, scales, pH meter, and sonicator.

--- Behavior Testing ---
Three dedicated behavior-testing rooms equipped with automated testing instruments, including: Med Associates automated operant-conditioning chambers for fear conditioning and drug self-administration, acoustic chambers, light-dark boxes, modular mazes, activity chamber, Rotarod for mice and rats with video-recording capabilities. Also includes manual open-field, Elevated Plus Maze, and T-Maze for mice and rats, plus equipment for small aquatic species.

--- Molecular Laboratory ---
Molecular laboratory equipped with: a Hacker Bright cryostat; a Keyence BZ-XB fluorescence microscope with live-cell imaging and automated cell-counting capabilities; plate washer; Countess cell counter; NanoDrop Lite; gel electrophoresis; refrigerated centrifuge; digital temperature shaking incubator; Wes(TM) Simple Western blot protein analysis apparatus; Molecular Devices Emax-Plus microplate reader; and small laboratory items (vortex mixer, centrifuge, plate washers, refrigerator, etc.).

Access to equipment is coordinated through the iLab Solutions service-center page (https://my.ilabsolutions.com/service_center/show_external/6195/research_infrastructure_core) and/or MSU's Microsoft Bookings link. Investigators must be IACUC-trained and have a current protocol approval to use animal-related equipment. Contact: Rysheem McGirt, Manager (Rysheem.Mcgirt@morgan.edu).
